question.text: Meera invests Rs.5,000 per month from age 30 to age 60 at an expected return of 12% per annum. What is the approximate corpus she will accumulate?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Using FV function: FV(12%/12, 360, -5000, 0, 0) = Rs.1,74,74,820. The monthly compounding at 12% for 30 years on Rs.5,000/month gives this result. **The Trap:** Rs.1,13,02,439 is the value at 10% return (not 12%). Rs.87,94,000 would be for about 25 years. Always check the rate and period carefully. **Cross-Exam Context:** Corpus accumulation calculations appear in Series V-A (SIP returns), X-A (financial planning), and X-B (retirement corpus estimation). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Show clients the power of compounding with this example — Rs.18 lakh total contribution grows to Rs.1.75 crore over 30 years at 12%.
